title: Fix misleading SIM107 documentation example
body:
## Summary
- The "Use instead" example for SIM107 had `return_value = -1` in the `finally` block, which always overwrites whatever was set in `try` or `except` — making the "corrected" code behave identically to the bad example (always returning -1).
- Remove the `finally` block from the "Use instead" example so it actually demonstrates the correct pattern: assign in `try`/`except`, then return after the block.

Closes #22325

## Test plan
- Visual inspection of the doc comment change.
- No behavioral code changes; only documentation fix.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
author: Chessing234

linked_issues.title: SIM107 documentation example may be misleading

issue_comments.body: Can you please disclose what work you did or is this PR all AI generated? Did you review the changes. Please read our AI Policy (https://github.com/astral-sh/.github/blob/main/AI_POLICY.md)
This is not equivalent. `return` within `finally` swallows exceptions. The example is correct as-is.

label_notes: AI-generated trivial doc-fix that misunderstood Python semantics. Chessing234 (prior_prs=1, NONE) submitted -2/+0 doc-only change removing finally block from SIM107 'Use instead' example, claiming the original was 'misleading' because finally always overwrites return_value. Body has explicit 🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code) footer. MichaReiser (MEMBER) immediately requested AI disclosure per AI_POLICY.md. Hnasar (CONTRIBUTOR) ultimately corrected: 'This is not equivalent. return within finally swallows exceptions. The example is correct as-is.' Classic AI-generated PR pattern: confident technical claim that misses subtle language semantics (try/finally interaction with exception propagation), proposes fix that breaks the example's pedagogical purpose. Adds: (a) AI-policy-violation + technical-incorrectness combination to slop class, (b) Claude Code footer pattern in ruff repo (different from ghostty#10515/#10614 same footer pattern), (c) doc-only AI-generated slop variant — small diff size doesn't preclude slop classification.
